_meta.source: MITRE ATT&CK Enterprise — curated subset for DFIR investigations

T1003.name: OS Credential Dumping
T1003.description: Adversaries dump credentials from OS or applications.

T1003.001.name: LSASS Memory
T1003.001.description: Dump credentials from LSASS process memory.

T1003.003.name: NTDS
T1003.003.description: Extract NTDS.dit Active Directory database for offline credential analysis.

T1003.006.name: DCSync
T1003.006.description: Abuse Directory Replication Services to retrieve credentials remotely.

T1021.name: Remote Services
T1021.description: Use legitimate remote services to move laterally.

T1021.002.name: SMB/Windows Admin Shares
T1021.002.description: Use SMB admin shares (C$, ADMIN$, IPC$) for lateral movement.

T1027.name: Obfuscated Files or Information
T1027.description: Make file content harder to analyze through encoding, encryption, packing.

T1036.name: Masquerading
T1036.description: Manipulate features of artifacts to make them appear legitimate.

T1036.005.name: Match Legitimate Name or Location
T1036.005.description: Use names or paths that match legitimate system locations.

T1041.name: Exfiltration Over C2 Channel
T1041.description: Send stolen data over an existing C2 channel.

T1048.name: Exfiltration Over Alternative Protocol
T1048.description: Exfiltrate data using a different protocol than the C2 channel.

T1053.name: Scheduled Task/Job
T1053.description: Use task schedulers for execution and persistence.

T1053.005.name: Scheduled Task
T1053.005.description: Use Windows Task Scheduler for persistence.

T1055.name: Process Injection
T1055.description: Inject code into a running process to evade defenses.

T1055.001.name: DLL Injection
T1055.001.description: Inject a DLL into a running process.

T1055.012.name: Process Hollowing
T1055.012.description: Unmap a legitimate process and inject malicious code in its place.

T1059.name: Command and Scripting Interpreter
T1059.description: Use command-line interpreters to execute commands.

T1068.name: Exploitation for Privilege Escalation
T1068.description: Exploit a vulnerability to elevate privileges.

T1070.name: Indicator Removal
T1070.description: Delete or modify generated artifacts to hide activity.

T1070.001.name: Clear Windows Event Logs
T1070.001.description: Clear Windows event logs to hide activity.

T1070.006.name: Timestomp
T1070.006.description: Modify file timestamps to confuse analysis.

T1078.name: Valid Accounts
T1078.tactic: Defense Evasion Persistence Privilege Escalation Initial Access
T1078.description: Use compromised credentials of legitimate accounts.

T1078.002.name: Domain Accounts
T1078.002.tactic: Defense Evasion Persistence Privilege Escalation Initial Access

T1090.name: Proxy
T1090.description: Use proxy servers to obscure C2.

T1110.name: Brute Force
T1110.description: Attempt to guess credentials by trial and error.

T1133.name: External Remote Services
T1133.description: Leverage external remote services (VPN, Citrix, RDP) for access.

T1505.name: Server Software Component
T1505.description: Abuse server software components for persistence.

T1505.003.name: Web Shell
T1505.003.description: Plant a web shell on a compromised web server.

T1543.name: Create or Modify System Process
T1543.description: Create or modify system processes for persistence.

T1543.003.name: Windows Service
T1543.003.description: Create a Windows service for persistence.

T1547.name: Boot or Logon Autostart Execution
T1547.description: Configure programs to run on boot or login.

T1547.001.name: Registry Run Keys / Startup Folder
T1547.001.description: Use Run keys or Startup folders for persistence.

T1560.name: Archive Collected Data
T1560.description: Compress and/or encrypt collected data prior to exfiltration.

T1560.001.name: Archive via Utility
T1560.001.description: Use utilities (rar, 7z, zip) to archive data.

T1562.name: Impair Defenses
T1562.description: Disable or interfere with security tools.

T1569.002.name: Service Execution
T1569.002.description: Execute payloads via newly created or modified services.

T1574.002.name: DLL Side-Loading
T1574.002.description: Side-load a malicious DLL via a legitimate binary.
